steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures: independent clauses with a transition word
steps.content: The first part (“Many works of the Greek historian Strabo are lost”) is an independent clause. The second part (“his Geographica is an extant work”) is also an independent clause. The word “conversely” acts as a transition between them.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content: When a transition word joins independent clauses, it must be preceded by a period or semicolon, never just a comma. A correctly uses “lost. Conversely,” with a period. B, C, and D violate this rule by using commas alone or creating malformed structures with “and.” That matches choice A — A is correct.
